text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY -- CLINICAL TRIAL ON-TREATMENT ASSESSMENT
Cycle 1, Week 4 Follow-Up Visit
Service: Medical Oncology / Thoracic Oncology / Clinical Trials Unit

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Ms. Yuen is a 64-year-old Chinese-American woman with Stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant pulmonary adenocarcinoma, now with systemic and intracranial progression, presenting for her scheduled cycle 1 week 4 on-study visit on the Phase I/II clinical trial evaluating the investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody (ABBV-CLS-484) in combination with a novel anti-PD-L1 agent. Today's visit is primarily a safety and tolerability assessment, with protocol-mandated laboratory evaluations, ECG, and clinical status review. Restaging imaging is not yet due.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Yuen is well known to our thoracic oncology service. For context: she was initially diagnosed with Stage IIIB (T3N2M0) right upper lobe pulmonary adenocarcinoma harboring an EGFR exon 21 L858R point mutation approximately two years ago at age 62. At that time, mediastinal staging confirmed multi-station N2 involvement (stations 4R and 7) by PET-CT and EBUS-guided biopsy. She declined concurrent chemoradiation due to concerns about her underlying COPD (GOLD Stage I) and proceeded instead with upfront osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily, achieving a substantial partial response by 3 months with continued deepening through 9 months (primary mass down from 4.8 cm to 1.8 cm at nadir, mediastinal nodes normalized). She remained on osimertinib for approximately 14 months before developing radiographic progression with enlargement of the primary mass (back up to 3.2 cm), new right paratracheal adenopathy, a small right pleural effusion, and two new brain metastases (1.1 cm right frontal lobe, 0.8 cm right cerebellum).

At that time, liquid biopsy via Guardant360 demonstrated acquisition of an EGFR T790M mutation at low VAF (1.6%), and tissue re-biopsy was deferred initially. She underwent stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) to both brain lesions using the Varian Edge system (20 Gy in 1 fraction to the right frontal lesion, 22 Gy in 1 fraction to the cerebellar lesion), then transitioned to second-line systemic therapy with carboplatin (AUC 5) plus pemetrexed (500 mg/m2) plus bevacizumab (15 mg/kg) every 21 days. She completed 4 cycles with confirmed partial response systemically and intracranial treatment response post-SRS, then transitioned to maintenance pemetrexed/bevacizumab.

During maintenance, she developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy attributed to prior carboplatin (managed with duloxetine 60 mg daily), grade 1 hypertension on bevacizumab (managed with addition of amlodipine 5 mg daily), and partial alopecia. She achieved stable disease on maintenance for approximately 4-5 months before developing her second progression: the primary mass enlarged from 2.2 cm to 3.8 cm, new bilateral pulmonary nodules appeared (right lower lobe 0.8 cm, left lower lobe 0.6 cm), and the previously SRS-treated right frontal lesion enlarged to 1.4 cm with surrounding edema. MRI perfusion and spectroscopy at that time favored true tumor recurrence over radiation necrosis (elevated rCBV, elevated choline:NAA ratio).

Maintenance chemotherapy was discontinued and she underwent repeat SRS to the right frontal lesion (18 Gy in 1 fraction, reduced dose given prior irradiation). She was started on a dexamethasone taper (4 mg BID, tapered over 3 weeks, now complete as of this visit). Tissue re-biopsy of the right upper lobe was performed for clinical trial eligibility and sent for comprehensive genomic profiling (Foundation One CDx), which demonstrated: EGFR L858R confirmed (VAF 48%), EGFR T790M now at higher tissue VAF (22%), and -- critically -- high-level MET amplification (copy number 6.8), confirming MET amplification as a bypass resistance mechanism. RB1 p.Q670* nonsense mutation was newly detected. PD-L1 TPS on re-biopsy: 28% (increased from initial 15%).

Given the EGFR L858R/T790M co-mutation plus MET amplification profile, the patient was identified as an excellent candidate for the EGFR/MET bispecific antibody trial, which specifically targets this dual resistance mechanism. She signed informed consent with her daughter present and initiated cycle 1, day 1 approximately 4 weeks ago.

Today, she returns for her week 4 on-study visit. She reports the following since her last visit:

-- FATIGUE: She endorses persistent grade 1 fatigue, which she rates as 3/10 in severity. She is able to perform most activities of daily living independently but notes that she tires more quickly than she did during her best response period on osimertinib. She does not feel this has worsened since initiating trial therapy.

-- HEADACHES: She reports that the bifrontal headaches that prompted evaluation several months ago have substantially improved since the repeat SRS and completion of the dexamethasone taper. Headaches are now mild and intermittent, rated 2/10 in severity, managed adequately with acetaminophen as needed (using approximately 2-3 times per week). She denies new or worsening neurologic symptoms. No new focal deficits. No seizure activity. No visual changes. No nausea or vomiting related to intracranial pressure.

-- DYSPNEA: She notices a slight improvement in her exertional dyspnea since starting trial therapy -- she is now able to walk approximately 1-2 blocks on flat ground before experiencing shortness of breath, compared to about half a block at the nadir of her second progression. She still uses an albuterol inhaler intermittently for her underlying COPD, roughly 2-3 times per week. No wheezing reported. No significant cough. No hemoptysis.

-- INFUSION-RELATED REACTION (PRIOR CYCLE): During cycle 1 day 1 administration of the investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody, she developed a grade 1 infusion-related reaction manifested as mild flushing, mild rigors, and heart rate elevation to 102 bpm during the infusion. The infusion was temporarily paused, she received additional diphenhydramine and acetaminophen per protocol, and the infusion was restarted without further incident. Per the protocol, cycle 1 day 1 of the next administration (cycle 2, day 1) will include enhanced pre-medication with dexamethasone 8 mg IV, diphenhydramine 50 mg IV, and acetaminophen 650 mg PO prior to infusion, with a slower initial infusion rate per the dose modification guidelines.

-- NAUSEA/APPETITE: She reports mild nausea, approximately grade 1, primarily in the morning. She is maintaining her oral intake adequately; eating 2-3 small meals daily. No vomiting. No significant weight loss since initiating trial therapy (reports her weight is roughly stable).

-- PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY: She continues to have grade 1-2 numbness and tingling in bilateral feet, unchanged from the neuropathy attributed to prior carboplatin therapy. She is tolerating duloxetine 60 mg daily, which she feels has provided modest improvement. No worsening or spread of neuropathy symptoms.

-- SKIN/DERMATOLOGIC: No new rash. The acneiform rash from her prior osimertinib therapy has resolved completely. No paronychia at this time.

-- BOWEL/BLADDER: Mild constipation (1 bowel movement every 2-3 days), which she attributes to reduced activity and diet changes. Using over-the-counter MiraLax with moderate effect. No diarrhea. Urination normal.

-- MUSCULOSKELETAL: No new bone pain. No back pain suggestive of bone metastases. No joint pain or myalgia.

-- MOOD/PSYCHOSOCIAL: She has been meeting with the social worker assigned to her by our clinical trials team. She lives at home with her daughter and son-in-law, which she says has been a great source of support. She is anxious about the upcoming restaging imaging (scheduled at cycle 3, approximately 2 weeks from today) but reports that her family's presence helps her manage the anxiety. She has asked about the possibility of acupuncture for fatigue and peripheral neuropathy; integrative oncology referral was placed at a prior visit and she is waiting for that appointment.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive: Fatigue (grade 1, stable), mild intermittent headaches (improved from prior), mild exertional dyspnea (improved), mild morning nausea, constipation, bilateral foot numbness/tingling (stable, carryover from prior carboplatin).
Negative: No fever, no chills (outside of the prior infusion-related reaction), no night sweats, no hemoptysis, no chest pain, no palpitations, no syncope, no new focal neurologic deficits, no seizure activity, no visual changes, no diplopia, no dysphagia, no significant diarrhea, no hematuria, no dysuria, no new rash, no arthralgias, no myalgias, no limb swelling, no new lymphadenopathy noted by patient.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1. Stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant non-small cell lung cancer (pulmonary adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe) -- as above, currently on Phase I/II clinical trial (third-line setting)
2. Hypertension -- well-controlled on current antihypertensive regimen
3. Type 2 diabetes mellitus -- well-controlled; last HbA1c 6.9%; on metformin; monitoring ongoing (note: dexamethasone taper now complete, glucose levels should normalize from steroid-related transient elevation)
4. Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, GOLD Stage I -- managed with albuterol PRN; no prior hospitalizations for exacerbation
5. Prior intracranial metastases: right frontal lobe (SRS x2) and right cerebellar hemisphere (SRS x1), now stable/treated
6. Carboplatin-induced peripheral neuropathy, grade 1-2 (ongoing)
7. History of EGFR TKI-related acneiform rash (grade 2, resolved) and paronychia (grade 1, resolved) during osimertinib

PAST ONCOLOGIC TREATMENT HISTORY (DETAILED):
1. First-line: Osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg PO daily -- ~14 months; discontinued for systemic and intracranial progression
2. Stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) #1: Right frontal (20 Gy/1 fx) and right cerebellum (22 Gy/1 fx) -- Varian Edge
3. Second-line: Carboplatin AUC 5 + pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 + bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q21d x4 cycles
4. Maintenance: Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 + bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q21d -- ~6 months, then discontinued for progression
5. Stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) #2: Right frontal lobe recurrence/progression, 18 Gy/1 fx
6. Current (third-line, clinical trial): Phase I/II investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody + anti-PD-L1 -- Cycle 1, Week 4

SURGICAL HISTORY: No prior thoracic surgery. No prior craniotomy. CT-guided core biopsy x2 (right upper lobe), EBUS-FNA (stations 4R and 7). No other surgeries of note.

SCOCIAL HISTORY: Born and raised in San Francisco, CA. Retired high school chemistry teacher. Lives with daughter and son-in-law. Former smoker, 20 pack-years, quit approximately 12-13 years ago. No current tobacco use, no alcohol use reported. Chinese-American descent. Has a strong family support system; daughter is present at most appointments.

FAMILY HISTORY: Mother with breast cancer (diagnosed age 70s, survived). Father with coronary artery disease and hypertension. No known family history of lung cancer, colorectal cancer, or other primary malignancies. No known germline mutation syndrome in the family.

ALLERGIES:
- No known drug allergies
- No known food allergies
- Investigational agent (EGFR/MET bispecific): Grade 1 infusion-related reaction on cycle 1 day 1 (managed with protocol pre-medications; not a formal allergy)

MEDICATIONS:
1. Investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody (ABBV-CLS-484) -- per trial protocol, IV, scheduled dosing
2. Investigational anti-PD-L1 agent -- per trial protocol, IV, scheduled dosing
3. Metformin 1000 mg PO twice daily (type 2 diabetes)
4. Amlodipine 5 mg PO daily (hypertension, added during bevacizumab maintenance)
5. Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily (hypertension -- baseline)
6. Albuterol HFA inhaler 90 mcg/actuation, 1-2 puffs PRN (COPD)
7. Duloxetine 60 mg PO daily (peripheral neuropathy)
8. Folic acid 400 mcg PO daily (residual from pemetrexed supplementation regimen; to continue per oncology team)
9. Vitamin B12 1000 mcg IM monthly (last injection given approximately 3 weeks ago; schedule to continue)
10. Acetaminophen 500-1000 mg PO PRN (headaches, mild pain; using 2-3x/week)
11. MiraLax (polyethylene glycol) 17 g PO daily PRN (constipation)
12. Ondansetron 4 mg PO PRN (nausea, morning use primarily)
13. Lubricating eye drops (OTC, bilateral dry eyes -- residual from osimertinib; using PRN)
14. Dexamethasone 4 mg BID -- COMPLETED taper (last dose approximately 3 weeks ago)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vital Signs: Blood pressure 128/76 mmHg (well-controlled on current regimen), heart rate 74 bpm and regular, respiratory rate 16 breaths/min, temperature 98.2 degrees F, oxygen saturation 95% on room air (stable for patient with COPD, baseline low-90s to mid-90s), weight 58.4 kg (stable, no significant change from last visit), BMI 22.1 kg/m2, height 163 cm.

General: Alert and oriented, well-appearing, no acute distress. Appears somewhat fatigued but conversational and cooperative. She is appropriately dressed and accompanied by her daughter.

HEAD AND NECK: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Pupils equal, round, and reactive to light bilaterally (3 mm to 2 mm). Extraocular movements intact. No nystagmus. No papilledema on fundoscopic exam (fundoscopy limited but posterior pole appears normal). Mild conjunctival pallor consistent with known mild anemia. No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis, no thrush. Neck supple, no cervical lymphadenopathy, no supraclavicular lymphadenopathy palpable bilaterally. Thyroid not enlarged.

LYMPH NODES: No axillary lymphadenopathy. No inguinal lymphadenopathy.

RESPIRATORY/CHEST: Breathing is unlabored at rest. Chest expansion symmetric. Percussion: dull at right base but less than prior examination; right pleural effusion clinically appears resolved or minimal, consistent with prior imaging. Auscultation: Diminished breath sounds at right base, mild scattered expiratory wheezes bilaterally consistent with underlying COPD. No crackles or rales suggesting pneumonitis. No stridor.

CARDIOVASCULAR: Regular rate and rhythm. S1 and S2 present, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No elevated JVP. No peripheral edema in the lower extremities. Peripheral pulses 2+ throughout.

ABDOMEN: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No organomegaly palpable (liver edge not palpated, no splenomegaly). No ascites clinically. Bowel sounds present and normoactive.

NEUROLOGICAL: Alert and oriented to person, place, and time. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Strength 5/5 in upper and lower extremities bilaterally. No pronator drift. No dysmetria on finger-nose or heel-shin testing. Gait is steady, though slightly cautious (patient uses her daughter's arm for reassurance). No ataxia on heel-to-toe walk. Sensation: Decreased to light touch and pinprick in bilateral feet (toes and dorsal foot, approximately to mid-foot level), consistent with known peripheral neuropathy. Reflexes 2+ at biceps, triceps, and brachioradialis bilaterally. Patellar reflexes diminished bilaterally (1+), Achilles reflexes trace bilaterally -- consistent with peripheral neuropathy pattern. No Babinski sign. No clonus.

SKIN: No acneiform or maculopapular rash. No paronychia. No petechiae or purpura. No significant skin changes at IV access sites. Mild alopecia (partial), consistent with prior chemotherapy exposure.

ECOG PERFORMANCE STATUS: 2 (ambulatory, capable of self-care, up and about more than 50% of waking hours, but unable to carry on any work activities).

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (PROTOCOL-MANDATED, OBTAINED THIS VISIT):
Complete Blood Count:
-- WBC: 4.2 x10^9/L (normal; no leukopenia)
-- ANC: 2.8 x10^9/L (adequate; no significant neutropenia)
-- Hemoglobin: 10.1 g/dL (grade 1 anemia, persistent from prior chemotherapy; stable from last visit of 10.3 g/dL)
-- Platelets: 198 x10^9/L (normal)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
-- Sodium: 137 mEq/L, Potassium: 4.1 mEq/L, Chloride: 102 mEq/L, CO2: 26 mEq/L (normal electrolytes)
-- BUN: 16 mg/dL, Creatinine: 0.82 mg/dL, eGFR >60 mL/min/1.73m2 (normal renal function)
-- Total bilirubin: 0.7 mg/dL (normal)
-- AST: 28 U/L, ALT: 34 U/L (within normal limits; no hepatotoxicity signal)
-- Alkaline phosphatase: 88 U/L (normal; no evidence of hepatic or bony disease progression by biochemistry)
-- Albumin: 3.6 g/dL (mildly low-normal, consistent with nutritional challenge of advanced malignancy)
-- Total protein: 6.8 g/dL

Endocrine/Metabolic:
-- Fasting glucose: 138 mg/dL (mildly elevated; was higher during dexamethasone taper, now improving toward her usual baseline; will continue to monitor given type 2 diabetes; note: dexamethasone taper now complete, glucose expected to continue improving)
-- HbA1c: not repeated this visit (last 6.9%); will recheck in ~6 weeks per primary care agreement
-- TSH: 2.1 mIU/L (normal; checked per protocol to screen for anti-PD-L1 thyroid-related toxicity; baseline value)

Lipid panel: Not obtained this visit.

Trial-specific labs (per protocol):
-- LDH: 198 U/L (slightly above upper limit of normal at this institution [ULN 185 U/L]; modest elevation; not unexpected in setting of known progressive disease; will trend)
-- Amylase: 42 U/L (normal)
-- Lipase: 38 U/L (normal)
-- Coagulation studies (PT/INR, aPTT): Within normal limits
-- Urinalysis (dipstick): No proteinuria, no blood, no glucose

Protocol-mandated ECG (obtained today):
-- Normal sinus rhythm, rate 74 bpm
-- PR interval 164 ms (normal)
-- QRS 90 ms (normal)
-- QTc (Fridericia): 432 ms (normal; within acceptable range for trial continuation per protocol eligibility thresholds; no QTc prolongation signal with investigational agent noted to date)
-- No ST or T wave abnormalities

Protocol-mandated echocardiogram (obtained this visit, per cycle 1 protocol requirement):
-- Left ventricular ejection fraction: 60-65% (normal)
-- No wall motion abnormalities
-- No pericardial effusion
-- Mild left atrial enlargement, unchanged from prior
-- No significant valvular pathology

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
No new imaging obtained at this visit. Per protocol, restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain are scheduled at cycle 3 (approximately 2 weeks from today). Last imaging reviewed:

-- CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (most recent, at time of second progression, prior to trial initiation): Right upper lobe mass 3.8 cm, bilateral pulmonary nodules (RLL 0.8 cm, LLL 0.6 cm).
-- MRI brain (most recent, perfusion/spectroscopy, at time of second progression): Right frontal lesion 1.4 cm with surrounding edema, favoring recurrence; right cerebellar lesion stable/resolved. Right frontal lesion subsequently treated with repeat SRS (18 Gy/1 fx) prior to trial initiation.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Ms. Yuen is a 64-year-old woman with Stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant pulmonary adenocarcinoma with acquired osimertinib resistance mediated by concurrent EGFR T790M and MET amplification, having now completed one cycle of an investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody combined with anti-PD-L1 immunotherapy on a Phase I/II clinical trial. She is presenting for her week 4 on-study safety and tolerability assessment.

Overall, her first cycle has been tolerated reasonably well. Key issues to address today are as follows:

1. CLINICAL TRIAL CONTINUATION AND TOLERABILITY -- Grade 1 infusion-related reaction during cycle 1 day 1 was the primary adverse event of note. This is a well-recognized reaction pattern with bispecific antibody constructs due to cytokine release. Going forward, enhanced pre-medication regimen has been added (dexamethasone 8 mg IV, diphenhydramine 50 mg IV, acetaminophen 650 mg PO) per protocol amendment, with a slower initial infusion rate. No dose modification is required at this time. She remains protocol-eligible. Cycle 2 is planned to proceed on schedule. The trial team will be notified of today's clinical summary and labs per required protocol communication. All grade 1 adverse events (fatigue, nausea, grade 1 infusion-related reaction) will be documented in the electronic study data capture system.

2. INTRACRANIAL STATUS AND HEADACHES -- The patient reports significant improvement in headaches following repeat SRS and completion of dexamethasone taper. The absence of new neurologic symptoms is reassuring. Dexamethasone taper is now complete, which is favorable from a glucose management perspective in the setting of her diabetes. Cycle 3 restaging MRI brain will provide the next formal intracranial assessment. If the right frontal lesion is stable or responding post-repeat SRS, we will continue to monitor on the standard brain MRI schedule. If there is concern for further enlargement at that time, neurosurgical consultation will be revisited. No interventions needed today for intracranial disease.

3. DYSPNEA -- Subjective improvement noted since initiating trial therapy. I am cautiously encouraged, but acknowledge that restaging imaging is the only reliable measure of pulmonary tumor response. Albuterol PRN for COPD exacerbations maintained. Oxygen saturation 95% at rest on room air is stable and acceptable for this patient's COPD baseline. I would counsel avoidance of exertion above tolerance and use of energy conservation strategies.

4. PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY -- Grade 1-2 bilateral foot numbness and tingling, stable, attributed to prior carboplatin exposure. Duloxetine 60 mg daily ongoing. Current investigational regimen does not include platinum chemotherapy or taxanes, so no expected additional neurotoxic burden from trial therapy. We will continue to monitor. Physical therapy referral previously discussed; will follow up with patient on whether she has connected with PT for balance and gait optimization.

5. ANEMIA -- Hemoglobin 10.1 g/dL, grade 1, stable/unchanged from prior. Not clinically symptomatic beyond contribution to fatigue. Iron supplementation was initiated during the prior chemotherapy phase; will confirm she is continuing oral ferrous sulfate and that tolerance is acceptable. EPO/erythropoiesis-stimulating agents are not indicated at this time given trial protocol restrictions (most bispecific antibody/immunotherapy trials restrict ESA use). Transfusion threshold for the outpatient setting remains hemoglobin <7.0-8.0 g/dL depending on symptoms.

6. DIABETES / HYPERGLYCEMIA -- Fasting glucose today 138 mg/dL, which is improved from readings during dexamethasone taper (peak readings in the 180s-190s per patient report). She has been monitoring at home per her endocrinologist's guidance. With dexamethasone taper now complete, glucose is expected to trend toward her usual controlled range on metformin. Will alert her primary care physician and endocrinologist of current labs via shared electronic medical record message. No change to diabetes regimen at this time.

7. NAUSEA AND CONSTIPATION -- Mild grade 1 morning nausea ongoing. Ondansetron PRN as tolerated. Will re-emphasize anti-nausea dietary strategies: small frequent meals, avoidance of strong food odors, ginger-containing products if tolerated. Constipation: MiraLax daily with adequate hydration counseled. If constipation not improved, can escalate to scheduled senna or docusate sodium.

8. PSYCHOSOCIAL / QUALITY OF LIFE -- ECOG PS remains 2, which is a decline from her best performance status of ECOG 0 during initial osimertinib response. This functional decline has been difficult for the patient and her family. Social worker is engaged and providing support services. Integrative oncology referral placed for acupuncture evaluation (fatigue and peripheral neuropathy). I counseled the patient that some degree of functional limitation is expected at this stage of her cancer trajectory, but that if the trial therapy achieves disease control, there is reasonable potential for improvement in symptoms and performance status. We discussed realistic expectations for this early phase of trial therapy: the primary goal at cycle 1 week 4 is safety and tolerability assessment, and definitive tumor response assessment will occur at cycle 3. I reassured her that her labs and cardiac function tests (ECG, echo) are within protocol parameters and that there are no safety signals requiring dose modification or trial discontinuation at this time.

9. UPCOMING PLAN:
 -- Cycle 2, Day 1: Schedule in approximately 10-14 days (per 21-day cycle schedule)
 -- Enhanced pre-medication per protocol (dexamethasone 8 mg IV, diphenhydramine 50 mg IV, acetaminophen 650 mg PO) to be confirmed with infusion nursing team prior to day 1 of cycle 2
 -- Slower initial infusion rate per protocol dose modification guidelines for prior grade 1 infusion-related reaction
 -- Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain: Cycle 3 (approximately 2 weeks from today; exact date to be confirmed with radiology scheduling)
 -- Repeat B12 injection: Due approximately next week; order placed
 -- Fasting lipid panel: Order for next visit
 -- Integrative oncology follow-up: Per referral order
 -- Physical therapy evaluation for balance/gait: Revisit at next visit
 -- Patient and daughter reminded to call trial nursing line for any new symptoms (fever, new neurologic symptoms, rash, worsening dyspnea, or any Grade 2+ adverse events) prior to next scheduled visit
 -- ECOG PS reassessment at cycle 2, day 1 and at cycle 3 restaging visit; if ECOG deteriorates to 3, protocol eligibility and trial continuation will need to be re-evaluated per the study protocol

Cancer status: Present, response to current therapy not yet assessable (too early, cycle 1 only).

Physician attestation: I personally evaluated the patient and reviewed all study documents, labs, ECG, and echocardiogram results. Trial-related adverse events documented per protocol in the electronic data capture system.

[Dictated and electronically signed by the treating thoracic oncologist, Clinical Trials Unit, Medical Oncology Service]
